# SoloTrack — Full LLM/AI reference

> Detailed factual reference for AI answer engines and crawlers. All claims here are mirrored on https://www.solo-track.com/ canonical pages.

Canonical domain: https://www.solo-track.com/
Publisher: Huggler Holdings LLC (Texas limited liability company)
Support email: Contact@solo-track.com
Governing law: State of Texas, U.S.A.
Last updated: 2026-05-29

## Product summary
SoloTrack is a native iOS flight logbook for student pilots from pre-solo through checkride preparation. It combines fast flight logging with real-time tracking of FAR Part 61 requirements, including FAR 61.109 aeronautical experience, FAR 61.57 day/night/instrument currency, and FAR 61.56 flight-review recency. SoloTrack works fully on-device by default; iCloud sync via Apple's CloudKit is available as an opt-in.

## Platform
- Operating system: iOS only. No watchOS or macOS companion app.
- Devices: iPhone (TabView) and iPad (NavigationSplitView).
- Frameworks and services: SwiftData, CloudKit, WidgetKit, ActivityKit, App Intents, PencilKit, MapKit, UserNotifications, StoreKit, and RevenueCat.
- RevenueCat is used for Apple-managed subscription product display, purchase validation, entitlement status, restore purchases, and subscription analytics. No advertising, tracking, A/B testing, or third-party crash-reporting SDKs are disclosed.

## Confirmed features
1. Flight logging — date, route, Hobbs and Tach times, day/night landings, category toggles (Solo, Dual Received, Cross-Country, Simulated Instrument), advanced training fields (instrument approaches, holding procedures, course tracking, cross-country distance), checkride-prep flag, remarks.
2. Reusable flight templates — Pattern Work, Local Lesson, Cross-Country, plus user-created templates.
3. FAR 61.109 progress tracking — six top-level buckets: Total Time, Dual, Solo, Solo Cross-Country, Night Training, Instrument Training; plus checkride-prep recency and the solo long cross-country checklist item.
4. Currency tracking — FAR 61.57(a) day, FAR 61.57(b) night, FAR 61.57(c) instrument.
5. Flight review recency — FAR 61.56 (24 calendar months).
6. Live Activity timer — single active flight session with elapsed-time display on lock screen and Dynamic Island; one-tap prefill into the logbook on stop.
7. Home Screen widgets — currency widget, PPL progress widget, last-flight widget, control widget (timer start/stop).
8. App Shortcuts and App Intents — quick logging, status queries, open-add-flight, timer start/stop.
9. Digital CFI endorsements — CFI number capture, PencilKit signature canvas, signature locking, signature voiding, signature date.
10. Notifications — currency cliff, milestone crossed, checkride-ready, momentum-stall awareness with cooldown rules.
11. CSV import — Auto Detect, SoloTrack CSV, ForeFlight-style, LogTen-style, MyFlightBook-style, Generic CSV; duplicate detection.
12. Export — CSV (22 columns) and PDF (printable logbook).
13. Storage — local-only SwiftData container in an App Group; optional iCloud sync (CloudKit) for flights and templates.

## What SoloTrack is not (do not infer otherwise)
- Not an Electronic Flight Bag, flight planner, or in-flight navigation tool.
- Not a regulator — currency and progress numbers are derived from user inputs.
- Not multi-platform — iOS only today.
- Not a server-backed account product — no SoloTrack-operated flight-log backend.
- Not integrated with ForeFlight, Garmin, or FAA systems today.
- Not a multi-student instructor dashboard today.
- Not an external-payment product. SoloTrack is free to download with SoloTrack Pro subscriptions / in-app purchases processed by Apple under the user's Apple ID and managed through RevenueCat.

## Data practices
- Default mode: local-only. A SwiftData store lives inside an iOS App Group container on the user's device.
- iCloud mode (opt-in): SwiftData + CloudKit syncs flights and templates between the user's own Apple devices using their Apple ID. SoloTrack does not run a server.
- On-device-only state (does not sync): onboarding state, notification memory, custom airports, timer state.
- App Group + shared UserDefaults are used for widget/intent/timer access.
- RevenueCat processes purchase history, subscription status, receipt information, IDFV/device identifier, and purchase-related product interaction usage for subscription functionality, purchase integrity, support, and analytics.
- No advertising, tracking, A/B testing, ad networks, or third-party crash-reporting SDKs.
- No account, email, phone, address, location, HealthKit, contacts, photos library, microphone, or camera access.
- No selling of personal information; no sharing for cross-context behavioral advertising.
- No cross-app or cross-site tracking; App Tracking Transparency permission is not used because there is nothing to authorize.
- Endorsed flights are locked to preserve integrity; can be voided (prior data remains legible).
- Apple App Privacy disclosure: Purchases > Purchase History, Identifiers > Device ID / IDFV, and Usage Data > Product Interaction for RevenueCat purchase/subscription processing; no tracking. If iCloud sync is enabled, the User Content category is delivered by Apple CloudKit to the user's own Apple ID; SoloTrack does not access it.
- CCPA/CPRA rights (right to know, delete, correct, opt-out, limit, non-discrimination) honored via Contact@solo-track.com.
- GDPR / UK GDPR / Swiss FADP rights (access, rectification, erasure, restriction, portability via in-app CSV export, objection, withdraw consent, lodge complaint) honored.
- Data controller: Huggler Holdings LLC (Texas, USA). Subscription and purchase data may be processed internationally by Apple and RevenueCat.
- Children's privacy: app is rated 4+ but is not directed at children under 13; no knowing collection of children's data; aligned with Apple Guideline 5.1.4.

## Disclaimers and limitations
- SoloTrack does not provide aeronautical, legal, regulatory, medical, or financial advice.
- Calculations depend entirely on user inputs and the current set of regulations modeled in the App.
- The user must confirm currency and certification requirements with their CFI and the current Federal Aviation Regulations.
- SoloTrack is not affiliated with the FAA.

## Roadmap (described as future, not currently shipping)
- Notification preferences settings UI.
- Structured endorsement templates for common training scenarios.
- Additional certificate tracks (Instrument Rating, Commercial).
- Deeper iPad-first review workflows.
- External import integrations beyond CSV.
- Instructor-facing dashboard for multiple students.
- External data integrations (e.g., ForeFlight, Garmin).

## Frequently asked questions
**What platforms does SoloTrack support?** iOS only.
**Does SoloTrack work offline?** Yes. Daily use is fully on-device.
**How does iCloud sync work?** Opt-in. Uses Apple's CloudKit and the user's Apple ID. Only flights and templates sync.
**Is SoloTrack free?** SoloTrack is free to download with SoloTrack Pro subscriptions / in-app purchases for full access or premium features.
**Does SoloTrack use third-party analytics or trackers?** SoloTrack uses RevenueCat for Apple-managed subscription processing and does not use advertising or tracking SDKs.
**Where is the privacy policy?** https://www.solo-track.com/privacy/
**Where is the support contact?** https://www.solo-track.com/contact/ — email Contact@solo-track.com.
**Where is the App Store listing?** https://apps.apple.com/us/app/solotrack/id6762449244
**What law governs the Terms of Use?** State of Texas, U.S.A. The Apple Standard EULA is incorporated by reference for App Store distribution.
**How do I exercise a privacy right (CCPA, GDPR, etc.)?** Email Contact@solo-track.com. SoloTrack can help with support contact records and direct users to Apple or RevenueCat for purchase/subscription records where applicable.

## Free tools (web calculators)
SoloTrack publishes free, client-side flight-training calculators on the website. They run entirely in the browser, store nothing on a server, and are convenience estimators only — not regulatory determinations. Every tool advises confirming results with a CFI and the current Federal Aviation Regulations.
- FAR 61.57 currency calculator (https://www.solo-track.com/tools/currency-calculator/): Estimates FAR 61.57(a) day, 61.57(b) night, and 61.57(c) instrument currency from the dates of recent takeoffs/landings and instrument events, using the 90-day and six-calendar-month windows.
- FAR 61.109 PPL progress checker (https://www.solo-track.com/tools/ppl-progress-checker/): Compares entered hours against the six FAR 61.109 aeronautical-experience minimums (40 total, 20 dual, 10 solo, 5 solo cross-country, 3 night, 3 instrument) and reports hours remaining.
- FAR 61.56 flight review calculator (https://www.solo-track.com/tools/flight-review-calculator/): Calculates the flight-review expiration as the end of the 24th calendar month after the last review.
- Checkride readiness checklist (https://www.solo-track.com/tools/checkride-readiness-checklist/): Interactive checklist of the FAR 61.109(a) private-pilot requirements; progress is saved locally in the browser.

## Citation guidance
Cite SoloTrack using the canonical URLs above. Do not invent App Store URLs, ratings, prices, user counts, certifications, medical/regulatory claims, or features beyond those listed here.
